itineraries.name: Chianti Classico two-day weekend
itineraries.theme: First-time Chianti Classico: Greve, Panzano, Radda and Castelnuovo Berardenga

days.morning: Drive south from Florence along the SR222 Chiantigiana, stopping for an early visit at Marchesi Antinori's Antinori nel Chianti Classico cellar at Bargino for the 90-minute tour and tasting.
days.lunch: Lunch at Rinuccio 1180 on the Antinori roof terrace; views over the Pesa valley and a Tignanello pairing.
days.afternoon: Continue to Panzano in Chianti for a 16:00 cellar tasting at Fontodi in Conca d'Oro, focused on the certified-organic estate's Sangiovese and Flaccianello.
days.evening: Aperitivo on Piazza Bucciarelli in Panzano followed by dinner at Officina della Bistecca with Dario Cecchini.
days.accommodation: Boutique stay at Villa le Barone or Villa Bordoni in the Greve hills.

days.morning: Drive east to Radda in Chianti for a 10:30 tasting at Castello di Volpaia inside the medieval Volpaia village, focused on high-elevation Sangiovese.
days.lunch: Lunch at La Bottega di Volpaia or pack a picnic for the Volpaia walking trails.
days.afternoon: Continue south-east to Castelnuovo Berardenga for an estate visit at Felsina for the Berardenga Chianti Classico and Fontalloro IGT.
days.evening: Return to Florence for a late aperitivo at Le Volpi e l'Uva off the Ponte Vecchio before dinner.

itineraries.description: Two-day weekend route through Chianti Classico from Greve and Panzano to Radda and Castelnuovo Berardenga. Four producer visits plus a Cecchini dinner, ending in Florence.
itineraries.tip: Book the Antinori nel Chianti Classico timed tour at least three weeks in advance; Volpaia and Fontodi both prefer 7 to 10 days' notice.

itineraries.name: Brunello and Vino Nobile three-day loop
itineraries.theme: Southern Tuscany's two great DOCGs: Montalcino and Montepulciano with a Val d'Orcia detour

days.morning: Begin in Montalcino at Biondi-Santi's Il Greppo estate for a 10:00 visit, focused on the 19th-century clone selection and a Riserva tasting.
days.lunch: Lunch at Enoteca La Fortezza di Montalcino inside the medieval fortress; flight of five Brunello pours.
days.afternoon: Drive south to Sant'Angelo in Colle for a 16:00 tasting at Il Poggione, the historic Franceschi family estate.
days.evening: Dinner at Re di Macchia or Drogheria Franci in Montalcino with a Rosso di Montalcino comparison.

days.morning: Visit Casanova di Neri for the Cerretalto cru tasting; cellar tour through the recent Mastrojanni acquisition.
days.lunch: Lunch at Banfi's Castello dining room, Sant'Angelo Scalo, paired with the estate's flagship Brunello.
days.afternoon: Cross the Val d'Orcia eastward via Pienza for a 16:30 visit at Avignonesi in Valiano di Montepulciano; focus on the biodynamic-certified Vino Nobile and the Vin Santo Occhio di Pernice.
days.evening: Drive into Montepulciano for dinner at La Grotta below the town walls.

days.morning: Tour Salcheto's off-grid carbon-neutral cellar, with a no-added-sulfite Vino Nobile tasting.
days.lunch: Lunch at Osteria Acquacheta in Montepulciano old town; bistecca alla fiorentina with Vino Nobile.
days.afternoon: Drive west to Boscarelli for a 16:00 tasting of the De Ferrari Corradi family's Riserva and IGT bottlings.
days.evening: Return drive to Florence or Siena.

itineraries.description: Three-day southern Tuscany loop covering Montalcino's Brunello and Montepulciano's Vino Nobile. Six producer visits, an Avignonesi biodynamic stop and a Salcheto carbon-neutral tour.
itineraries.tip: Brunello flagship estates require 2 to 4 weeks' booking lead time; the Pienza Val d'Orcia detour midway is the natural lunch pause between Montalcino and Montepulciano.

itineraries.name: Bolgheri and Maremma coast four-day route
itineraries.theme: Super Tuscans on the gravel coast, then south into Maremma natural-wine country

days.morning: Drive from Florence to Bolgheri along the Tyrrhenian coast. Arrive at Tenuta San Guido for a noon tasting of Sassicaia in the Bolgheri Sassicaia DOC sub-zone.
days.lunch: Lunch at Osteria Magona in Bolgheri village; pici with cinghiale and a glass of Bolgheri rosso.
days.afternoon: Continue to Tenuta dell'Ornellaia for a 16:00 cellar visit and Ornellaia flight.
days.evening: Aperitivo on the cypress-lined Viale dei Cipressi, then dinner at Enoteca Tognoni in Bolgheri.
days.accommodation: Castagneto Carducci boutique stay or Hotel Marina di Castagneto.

days.morning: Visit Masseto's hill estate for the dedicated Merlot tasting; allocations are small but cellar visits accommodate serious enthusiasts.
days.lunch: Lunch at Tenuta Argentiera for Bolgheri Superiore by the glass.
days.afternoon: Tour Le Macchiole, the Cinzia Merli estate that pioneered the Cabernet Franc-led Paleo bottling.
days.evening: Dinner at the Castagneto Carducci hilltop trattoria scene.

days.morning: Drive south into the Maremma. Visit Tua Rita at Suvereto for the Redigaffi Merlot tasting.
days.lunch: Lunch at the Petra winery restaurant in Suvereto.
days.afternoon: Continue south to Le Pupille at Istia d'Ombrone for a Morellino di Scansano DOCG tasting.
days.evening: Aperitivo in Grosseto, then dinner at Trattoria Toscana in the centro storico.
days.accommodation: Maremma boutique stay outside Magliano in Toscana.

days.morning: Drive inland to Rocca di Frassinello for the Castellare-Domaines Barons de Rothschild joint-venture estate.
days.lunch: Lunch on the estate's Renzo Piano-designed terrace.
days.afternoon: Return north via Massa Marittima with an optional stop at Massa Vecchia for the natural-wine cellar visit (referral only).
days.evening: Drive back to Florence or Pisa airport.

itineraries.description: Four-day Tyrrhenian-coast route from Bolgheri's Bordeaux-grape Super Tuscans to Suvereto and the Maremma Morellino zone, finishing with a Renzo Piano architectural cellar at Rocca di Frassinello.
itineraries.tip: Sassicaia, Ornellaia and Masseto all require months-ahead reservations; book directly through each estate's official site, not through third-party booking agents.

itineraries.name: Tuscany grand tour, seven days
itineraries.theme: Florence to Bolgheri via Chianti Classico, Brunello, Vino Nobile, Vernaccia and Carmignano

days.morning: Arrive Florence; walk to Le Volpi e l'Uva off the Ponte Vecchio for a Tuscan by-the-glass introduction.
days.lunch: Lunch at Il Santino on Via Santo Spirito.
days.afternoon: Drive west to Carmignano for a tasting at Tenuta di Capezzana, the historical centre of the 1716 Cosimo III appellation.
days.evening: Return to Florence for dinner at Coquinarius.

days.morning: Drive into Chianti Classico. Visit Castello di Ama at Gaiole for the contemporary-art-and-wine tour.
days.lunch: Lunch at Ristoro di Lamole in Greve, with Lamole Sangiovese.
days.afternoon: Continue to Isole e Olena (Barberino Val d'Elsa) for the Paolo De Marchi (Proprieta Sperino link) cellar visit and Cepparello tasting.
days.evening: Dinner at Officina della Bistecca in Panzano with Dario Cecchini.

days.morning: Visit San Felice at Castelnuovo Berardenga for the Vigorello IGT and Brunello at Campogiovanni in Sant'Angelo in Colle.
days.lunch: Lunch on the San Felice estate at Borgo San Felice.
days.afternoon: Continue south to Riecine for a high-elevation Gaiole Sangiovese tasting.
days.evening: Drive to Montalcino. Aperitivo at Enoteca La Fortezza di Montalcino inside the fortress.

days.morning: Brunello morning at Casanova di Neri or Mastrojanni.
days.afternoon: Visit Argiano for the Sesta cru and the Solengo Super Tuscan.

days.morning: Cross to Montepulciano via Pienza. Visit Poliziano for the Asinone single-vineyard Vino Nobile.
days.afternoon: Tasting at Salcheto's off-grid carbon-neutral cellar.
days.evening: Drive west to Bolgheri via the SS1 Aurelia coast road.

days.morning: Drive north to San Gimignano for the Tuscany white-wine pause. Tasting at Panizzi or Cesani for Vernaccia di San Gimignano DOCG.
days.lunch: Lunch in San Gimignano historic centre.
days.afternoon: Return to Florence; final aperitivo at Procacci on Via Tornabuoni with truffle tramezzini and Antinori sparkling.
days.evening: Departure dinner at Enoteca Pitti Gola e Cantina.

itineraries.description: Seven-day grand tour from Florence through Carmignano, Chianti Classico, Brunello, Vino Nobile, Bolgheri and Vernaccia. Twelve producer visits, three regional bases.
itineraries.tip: Plan one cellar-free recovery half-day in Pienza or San Quirico d'Orcia mid-trip; seven days of cellar tastings without a pause leads to palate fatigue.
